item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A clingy and overly possessive entity, really like this one. Would love to get an insight to the realm where the foreign objects are taken.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images were created by the author of the piece I rewrote and they gave me permission to use them here. I guarantee that the images are compliant. However, the proof would be somewhere in #site19 chat logs from eight years ago, so I understand if it'd be too cumbersome (or impossible) to find the creator's statement of permission and the images need to stay down.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article could not be sourced, and thus are assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some bad expungement (What would happen if you were to try to leave the room with the ghost there? would it transform into a dementor and suck out your soul?) But the overall result is fantastic, it falls spot on the thin line between scary and lovely, and that's something really hard to pull off. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the old-school spoopiness of this SCP. And as such, upvoted!

item.title: Done before, but...
item.content:encoded:
While this general type of haunting has been done before, I feel SCP-1170-2 conveys the tone of its unconditional "love" well, keeping a sense of mystery around exactly what that "love" will entail. Considering the various body fluids that were recovered, I don't think that I would chance it.

On the other hand, some of the redacted stuff seemed a little excessive, or unnecessary. For instance, why expunge mention of that last recovered body fluid?

Overall, I found it enjoyable, and the transcript of the audio log pushed it into a +1 from me.

EDIT: Apparently, I was the 100th positive. Neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. Here's some critique-type-words.

+ Show quibbles
- collapse

Exiting the room is not recommended while SCP-1170-2 is present in the room, as [DATA EXPUNGED].

To me, this feels like a cop-out, like there's nothing actually behind the expungement. It reads as if the author couldn't come up with a real reason so they just stuck a [DATA EXPUNGED] in there 'cause it's probably sumthin' real spoopy.

Yet I get the feeling it's something like this…

Exiting the room is not recommended while SCP-1170-2 is present in the room, as SCP-1170-2 will become emotionally distressed and de-manifest. For roughly four hours following de-manifestation, audio recording devices will register sounds identified as the song Fortress of Tears by Finnish rock band HIM, on loop, accompanied by quiet sobbing.

Poor feller.

And as for this:

the sudden emotional changes experienced by subjects conversing with the entity suggest that SCP-1170-2 may have a memetic effect.

That's not a memetic thing. See understanding-memetics

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kudos for using your own image instead of being all like, "Oh, hey! I could write a scip based on this random image I found on the interwebz! Sure hope it doesn't turn out to be copyrighted or something, herpty derp!"
…not that anyone's ever done that.

item.title: Meh
item.content:encoded: To be hones,t it doesn't seem remarkable: a cliche ghost, I did down vote, but obviously I don't need anyone to agree with me, nevertheless, it's just 'meh', nothing new or really strange, at least, not compared to other SCPs on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should have made that edit a long time ago… fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"..repels all visible wavelengths…"

I think that should say absorbed. Repeling and or emitting light is how objects are seen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy Crap……

I live in British Columbia…..

(._.')

+1 While I hide under my covers tonight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that was incredibly strange. I do not know how to feel, save to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1170-1 seems to repel all visible wavelengths

Kinda seems unscientific. If the room absorbs all visilble wavelengths, it will look dark and black. If the room reflects all visible wavelengths,it will probably look mirror-like. And anyway, light can be reflected or absorbed, not repelled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Area-1170 has been built around SCP-1170-1. The entire apartment complex has been blocked off; however, due to its rather public location

This is a Site, not an Area.

Sites are often built in locations in relatively close proximity to civilian populations…
Areas … are often built far away from civilian populations

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, it's got just the right amount of mystery and intrigue (what with the blood of the D-class appearing on the walls afterwards).

item.title: Re: How big?
item.content:encoded: You're very welcome! Also take my upvote. I wasn't withholding it, just forgot to give it before.

item.title: Re: How big?
item.content:encoded: No, that wasn't intended. I shrunk the room to a more reasonable size. Thanks.

item.title: How big?
item.content:encoded: Sorry, this is a very minor nitpick, but… the bedroom is how big now? 20 meters by 20 meters by 3.2 meters? That's about 64 feet on a side. You could fit a swimming pool in there. Is the enormous size of the room a part of the anomaly, like a House of Leaves type effect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I'd already upvoted this. Hrrm. I quite like this one, it all fits together nicely.

if I have another comment somewhere in here explaining a neutral vote remember that me from the past is stupid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I addressed some of that. Also, thank you Pineapple of Despair; I'm not sure how so many people (myself included) missed that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is kind of nitpicky, but:

The room cannot be illuminated by visible light (excluding the built-in light fixture), as the darkness within the room seems to repel all visible wavelengths.

1) Darkness is just the absence of light, so how could it do anything? The room, or the space within the room, could do something.

2) Saying the light is "repelled" is imprecise. Is the light reflected back? Does it seem to just disappear?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh… I can't help but feel I have read this one countless times before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always thought that picture of the little ghost thing was kinda cute. After the movie "Brave" came out, I thought the wisps kinda reminded me of this thing. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WHAT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Might I ask where?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I feel the redaction is quite clumsy, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Salvation and eternal love for doomed Disposables. Or possibly they get taken to the spirit world to be eaten. Either/or. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hahaha! Holy crap, you're right!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First picture is SCP-598, this time in green!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, since this was a rewrite, a lot of the struck through text was from the original article. I decided to keep it because it looked nice, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The audio log makes this entry. What's with all the entries recently with strikeout text? I know it's meant to demonstrate the evolution of the document as new observations are made, but it makes things look cluttered to me. I'm not really sure how to fix it without just removing that content though, and I do like seeing that previous content. Hmmmm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The chupracabra NEEDS YOU.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please define "work." If you see faulty wording or grammar/spelling mistakes, fixing them is fine. If you have a concept change or expansion of the concept in mind, please PM me before taking action.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got a little carried away, eh heh. (I started out just looking for the one word, but then I got hung up on some science errors…) If you don't like the changes, please feel free to revert them, and I'll go back and only edit the bit with the "spectre".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That being said, could probably use a little more work. I'll look over it a few times and make a few changes if you don't mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice rewrite. 90% of the information was kept the same, just condensed and phrased more clearly. The other 10% has given more information on the SCP instead of 100 [REDACTED]s.

I'm glad some people like the pictures, they were edited from scratch. Thanks for saving this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go for it! Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, yes, it's the acceptable UK spelling. However you spell it, though, "spectre" is not a particularly appropriate word for use in a technical document. May I take a whack at rephrasing it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those were both actually left over from the original article, but fix'd. (Isn't spectre an acceptable spelling?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Sporting" inverted joints? A "spectre"? This writeup has tone problems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really memetic.
Love the pictures.
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll say it again: A SPELL-CHECK IS NOT ENOUGH!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My less coherent complaints aside, this really doesn't seem memetic. The redaction and other stylistic elements don't help either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love you

+1

Though there are some redacted that annoy me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this started out as a rewrite of SCP-1170 as written by McNick, but it may have evolved into an entirely new thing. Feedback would be appreciated.
